title: PV Sindhu Dominates Han Yue, New 15-Point Format Favors Her
indian_angle: This report highlights PV Sindhu's strong performance against Han Yue and discusses how the proposed 15-point scoring system could benefit Indian players.
summary: PV Sindhu delivered a commanding performance against Han Yue, winning 21-16, 21-14, showcasing her adaptability and power. The article suggests that the potential shift to a 3x15 scoring format could particularly suit her aggressive style of play, making her a formidable contender in future tournaments.

title: India Finalizes Junior World Championships Selection Criteria
indian_angle: The BAI has issued crucial eligibility guidelines and confirmed a selection tournament for Indian players aiming for the BWF World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has released the eligibility criteria for Indian players aspiring to compete in the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo, Egypt. This follows the declaration of the Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament as the official selection event, after the postponement of the Haryana U-19 tournament.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System Implemented in Indian Domestic Badminton
indian_angle: This marks a significant change in domestic badminton, impacting how Indian players train and compete at junior and national levels.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has officially implemented the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships across U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups. This new format is set to debut at the Ernakulam tournament, aiming to bring a fresh dynamic to Indian junior badminton.

title: Delhi Prepares to Host BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: India is set to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in Delhi, a major event for the sport's growth and fan engagement in the country.
summary: Delhi is gearing up to host the BWF World Championships in 2026, an event that is expected to draw significant attention and boost badminton's popularity among younger generations in India. The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has already initiated an Expression of Interest (EOI) process for the organization of this global spectacle.

title: Chennai to Host All India Senior Ranking Tournament
indian_angle: This major domestic tournament in Chennai provides a crucial platform for India's senior badminton players to compete and improve their national rankings.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, scheduled to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, 2026. This event is a key fixture in the domestic calendar for senior players.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing his continued strong form on the international circuit.
summary: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open but ultimately fell short against Su Li Yang, finishing as the runner-up. His journey to the final highlighted his resilience and skill against top international competition.
